content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 17, 1987

Frank C. Damrell
Damrell, Damrell & Nelson
1625 I Street
Modesto, CA 95354

Re: California Collider Commission
Our File No. I-87-158

Dear Mr. Damrell:

On May 22, 1987, the Office of the Secretary of State received
an amendment to your firm's Lobbying Firm Registration Statement
(Form 601) adding the California Collider Commission as an entity to
which the firm will provide lobbying services. The amendment was
forwarded to this office for a determination of whether the
California Collider Commission qualifies as a lobbyist employer.
The Lobbying Firm Activity Authorization (Form 602) which was
completed by the Chairperson of the Collider Commission's Executive
Steering Committee contained the following statement:

This authorization is prepared for the sole purpose of ensuring
that the lobbying firm registration statement required by
California Government Code Section 86104 is complete.
California Government Code Section 86300 exempts the Executive
Steering Committee as an entity of the State of California from
all other lobbyist provisions of the Political Reform Act.

Section 86300(a) provides an exemption from the Act's lobbying
disclosure provisions for:

....any employee of the State of California acting within the
scope of his employment....
(Emphasis added.)

Employees of your lobbying firm are not employees of the State
of California. However, the exemption for employees of the State of
California may, under certain circumstances, apply to outside
consultants who contract to provide lobbying services to a state
agency.

Enclosed please find a copy of an advice letter issued to Sharon
Quessenberry of the law firm Turner & Sullivan (No. A-84-121). The
letter states that the exclusion in Government Code Section 86300
applies to a law firm and its employees who provide lobbying
services to a state agency so long as each employee who provides
lobbying services to the state agency is a "designated employee"
(Government Code Section 82019) and files statements of economic
interests under the conflict of interest code which the agency is
required to adopt (Government Code Sections 87300-87313).

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Frank C. Damrell
Page 2

Therefore, unless the employees of Damrell, Damrell and Nelson
who will provide lobbying services to the California Collider
Commission are designated in the Commission's conflict of interest
code and file statements of economic interests, the Commission must
file reports as a lobbyist employer. In addition, Damrell, Damrell
and Nelson must report on its quarterly Lobbying Firm Report (Form
625) the required information for a lobbyist employer in connection
with the services it provides to the Commission.

If you have any questions regarding the above, please contact me
at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Carla Wardlow
Carla Wardlow
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division

Enclosures
cc: William B. Baker,
     California Collider Commission
     Secretary of State

Division 
California Collider commission 
Secretary of State
sections.question_synthetic: Does the California Collider Commission qualify as a lobbyist employer requiring registration and reporting when Damrell, Damrell & Nelson provides lobbying services to it?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The California Collider Commission must file reports as a lobbyist employer unless the lobbying firm's employees are designated in the Commission's conflict of interest code and file statements of economic interests. The lobbying firm must also report required information on its quarterly reports.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: OCR document with some illegible sections (particularly page 2). No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections present; content extracted from body text. File number I-87-158. Document references an enclosure (advice letter A-84-121) for precedent. Some OCR errors visible but core message clear.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the California Collider Commission qualify as a lobbyist employer requiring registration and reporting when Damrell, Damrell & Nelson provides lobbying services to it?

CONCLUSION: The California Collider Commission must file reports as a lobbyist employer unless the lobbying firm's employees are designated in the Commission's conflict of interest code and file statements of economic interests. The lobbying firm must also report required information on its quarterly reports.

The Lobbying Firm Activity Authorization (Form 602) which was completed by the Chairperson of the Collider Commission's Executive Steering Committee contained the following statement: This authorization is prepared for the sole purpose of ensuring that the lobbying firm registration statement required by California Government Code Section 86104 is complete. California Government Code Section 86300 exempts the Executive Steering Committee as an entity of the State of California from all other lobbyist provisions of the Political Reform Act. Section 86300(a) provides an exemption from the Act's lobbying disclosure provisions for: ....any employee of the State of California acting within the scope of his employment.... (Emphasis added.) However, the exemption for employees of the State of California may, under certain circumstances, apply to outside consultants who contract to provide lobbying services to a state agency. The letter states that the exclusion in Government Code Section 86300 applies to a law firm and its employees who provide lobbying services to a state agency so long as each employee who provides lobbying services to the state agency is a "designated employee" (Government Code Section 82019) and files statements of economic interests under the conflict of interest code which the agency is required to adopt (Government Code Sections 87300-87313). 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Damrell Page 2 Therefore, unless the employees of Damrell, Damrell and Nelson who will provide lobbying services to the California Collider Commission are designated in the Commission's conflict of interest code and file statements of economic interests, the Commission must file reports as a lobbyist employer. In addition, Damrell, Damrell and Nelson must report on its quarterly Lobbying Firm Report (Form 625) the required information for a lobbyist employer in connection with the services it provides to the Commission. If you have any questions regarding the above, please contact me at (916) 322-5662. embedding.summary: The FPPC provides guidance to Damrell, Damrell & Nelson regarding whether the California Collider Commission qualifies as a lobbyist employer under the Political Reform Act. The Commission is subject to lobbying disclosure and reporting requirements unless the firm's employees are properly designated and comply with conflict of interest code provisions.
